reviews.review_text:
Game was bad, then game was good and now game is bad again.

When it first dropped it was rough but that's what early access is for so no hate there, trudge through and give feedback.

Then they got it running smooth and it was a blast especially with friends. Running missions with buddies was the peak experience for me. The game is still good solo but there is nothing like clearing a camp with the boys in my personal opinion.

So naturally they decided to ruin that experience. Whoever thought that it would be a good idea to introduce a system where you buddies can have theirs tasks all scattered and jumbled to the wind made a huge mistake. Basically it's already annoying enough to make sure you and your buddies stay on task together but that's part of the logistics and fun of it so you it's ok. Then comes one of their more recent updates and now if you Die you get reset to step 1 or just barred from doing that task altogether. Some tasks can have steps that take hours to complete so if you are running missions with your buddies and just one buddy dies which in this game can happen regardless of how slow you take things then that guy is now totally off kilter from the rest of the squad. So you try to pivot and just run a different set of missions but you find that another buddy is on  a different leg of that mission so it just goes to crap real fast.

Love the game but please please remove this punishment system for dying. It's already punishment enough to try and get back to your body without dying a second time, I would say especially in the PVE servers it makes no sense to have such a brutal punishment for death.

You will notice I still recommend the game, because it is truly a fun game but just be warned if you intend to play with friends for now as of 5/20/26 it's a rough ride.

I really like this game.

I only play PvE.

I really like the attention to detail, the atmosphere of moving through Lamang especially at night. Using Night vision with IR lasers has been extremely satisfying. Being able to take out an entire enemy squad and then fade back into the darkness of the woods to do it again further down the road.

They have different mechanics like most of the more advanced extraction shooters, cantering your weapon to use a visible or IR laser, zooming your scope, being able to crouch and control the heigh of your character to a very granular level so you can come up just enough to hit your target then drop back down and disappear.

I recommend reviewing and testing out all the key binds, find new binds for things you plan to use regularly. 

This game gives me nostalgia from games like Delta Force from the late 90s.

After 150 hours, this is my overall opinion. I really like the core idea of the game — the atmosphere, the map, and I was hoping the fights would be slower and more tactical. Unfortunately, that’s not the case.

The AI bots are either literal Terminators that kill you with a single bullet, or they do absolutely nothing except give away your position.

I honestly don’t know how they designed the armor system, but every time I start shooting, there are insane ricochets everywhere. One time I almost killed myself because of it.

As for people using cheats or third-party software, it’s honestly ridiculous. I have almost 9k hours in Escape from Tarkov, and in all that time I haven’t seen this many cheaters.

There are plenty of other issues too, but I don’t want to keep ranting for no reason. So, what I really want to say is this:
If you’re someone who enjoys PvP and wants good competitive fights, don’t waste your time right now. The game supposedly has anti-cheat, but it clearly isn’t working. If you plan to play PvE instead, then the experience is a bit better. Otherwise, I’d recommend waiting a little longer to see what happens with the game before investing more time into it — otherwise I’ll probably quit completely myself.
And quickly about optimization: I’m getting around 50–60 FPS most of the time on medium/low settings, and when I use a scope it drops to around 30 FPS.

For a long time, I was craving for a game like this, and after finding it, I'm totally in love with it!

Yeah, you can call it another milsim extraction shooter - pretty popular genre these days. Big accents on realism around gunfights - guns have realistic ballistics, sound is spectacular, and gun customisation is just something else. I think the possibility to tweak each part of the gun to my liking is the top reason why I like this game so much. It's not your usual attachment system like in most of the games; it's more like a constructor where you assemble different parts to make a perfect build. For example, the ACOG scope can't be easily mounted on a Picatinny rail, so you need to mount the ACOG adapter first. Most of the games ignore such details, as most gamers would not care about them, and it's totally fine - not every game needs to be so meticulous in details. But for realism and immersion purposes its a huge deal. The game gives the player big freedom in how to set up their gear - besides guns, you have lots of other ways to express yourself in your gear. It's your typical setup of plate carriers, helmets, and backpacks, each with its own pros and cons. Items provide you with grid-system slots to fill with ammo and other medical consumables, but do not forget to have some extra space for valuables. I just love tweaking all this stuff to my liking - how many magazines I need to have, which types of ammo to load into them, which meds to take, how to sort them to have easy access in case of wounds in the middle of a gunfight.

Besides realistic milsim gunplay, GZW also interested me with its world-building. It's not completed yet, as the game is still in EA and the game's story is developing as the game keeps updating, but what we have now is already intriguing. You are a mercenary working for a PMC deployed to a quarantined island in Asia. Your goal is to investigate what happened here, what caused the evacuation, and what is inside the mysterious Zero Ground zone in the middle of the island. Right now, there are not so many details to the story, especially with quests often relying on environmental storytelling and tons of text. What we have right now is a good beginning (that needs to be more fleshed out) and a promise of a great mystery with paranormal origins. Devs are talking about inspiration by “Roadside Picnic”, so we are waiting for some Stalker-like development in ZG.

GZW is still in active development - a lot of features are still work in progress, many mechanics are in the way to be added in future patches. Enemy AI still needs more improvements, stealth needs to be a viable option, balance between PvP and PvE needs to be tweaked, and overall, more content is wanted - more guns, more gear, more consumables, more valuables. The game has great potential and passionate devs, so it's easy for me to believe that in the future, this game will become a true masterpiece. Right now its great base of a game that is already fun to play and spend dozens of hours, while fighting other PMCs and local goons, investigating the story and worldbuilding, and working on your gear.

It's not the most optimized yet, but it's still in EA, so there are crashes and bugs, for sure. This is a solid experience other than that one hiccup, though. They've listened to the community to improve the experience where it was not strong and the world and story are immersive and interesting. The community on the PvE side is also an overall really wholesome experience (PvP I can't say, I haven't played).

My friend and I loved the PvE mode, but as soon as we tried a PvP server for a change of pace, we got instantly killed by an obvious cheater. Gave it a second chance later, but ran into the exact same issue. This game will never work if they do not instant ban these people more frequently.

I have had a lot more fun in the game since the 0.4 update.  It's nice to see a developer that listens to the players.  I only play PVE, I have never really enjoyed the PVP aspect.  As a PVE player, I do feel there are some things that really need to be done to improve the gameplay.  First, Faction only servers for PVE would drastically change the playability of the game in regards to missions.  Since we don't interact with the other factions, there is no point in fighting over locations with them.  I have gone in to complete a mission only for everything to be dead and cleared with other factions in the area that you can't see on the map.  Especially areas closer to their home base, Fort Narith for example.  Go in for the boss mission and everything is dead with 2-3 squads running around that you can't see on the map.  Either give them a different color designator on the map or just simply split us off.  The next thing would be to keep PVP and PVE loot separate.  Clearing an area only to have it looted by scavenge rats that run back off to PVP gets annoying.  Another game within the same genre does not allow the PVP/PVE loot to mix and we need that.  I would also like them to fix the stamina and arm pump.  Holding scoped in as an elite military fighting machine only to be out of breath after 10 seconds is a joke.

This game is incredibly fun. It's unfortunate that I will not continue playing into the next patch. Very disappointing that the developers hold a full staff meeting to discuss the patches for 42 minutes, yet fail to discuss the biggest problem that the game has. Cheating has made PvP unplayable, while burning out of PvE is only right around the corner. The game becomes repetitive without the unpredictability of PvP, and without a proper Anti-Cheat system in place this game will not be enjoyable for more than a few days. Do better MadFinger.

played smooth great game the anti cheat seems to be not working though got a 3 day ban for autorunning or something like that never even heard of such a thing , and all i was doing was traveling down a trail and bam banned get rid of that part great game though

The concept and gameplay here is fantastic; however, ESP cheating is increasing exponentially on this wipe. 

I have about 100+ hours in 4.0, I game with a group of friends. We only play Warfare (PvEvP) and every day we play, whether it's a weekday or weekend, we are running into at least one instance of suspected cheating. This is not a skill issue, I concede that I make mistakes and have the deaths to prove it. I never really question the close-quarters combat, it is the medium to long range that raises red flags for us. Our group has seen the aimbotting/esp get absolutely out of control. We've been shot in the middle of the woods by a guy on a helicopter (one tap). We've been fighting around Harrison / Bronco, had success and also failure. We've pushed a solo player from 3 different directions in the middle of the new darker nights, we were all one tapped in the head with full auto fire. The solo player snapped to our heads, through the forest, over 180 degree change in direction in split seconds. We've observed players running through fields while we sit in tree lines, as soon as we aim the player will stop and snap to us and start shooting. We've been hiding around a COP, completely stationary for minutes, then get shot from behind while completely concealed.

This is killing the PVP mode, and without it there's no real reason to play the game. You can just search up GZW ESP on google and find youtube videos of people using these cheats. Blatant Aimbot, ESP, spectating camera, no spread accuracy, accurate minimap showing everyones line of site around you, ESP overlay showing if the other player can see you or not. The discord group for that ESP(Devs can dm me for the name, don't want them to give any more publicity that they already have) had over 2700 players in it with 500+ people online actively discussing gray zone warfare cheats. PVE is okay, the AI is engaging, but how long can someone just loot/task until it's boring? Once you max traders out, you're just running around and shooting the ai so you can grab some loot. Madfinger, please do something to combat these guys.

Grey Zone Warfare PvP is amazing if you enjoy fighting Professor X-level psychic operators with gaming chairs blessed by God himself.

Nothing says “legit player” like a level 7 fresh account instantly knowing your exact location through 14 bushes, 3 walls, darkness, rain, and your entire bloodline history. Then they kill you and hit you with the classic:
“bro I’m just good.”

Yeah okay buddy, apparently you downloaded ultra instinct with your starter edition.

The funniest part is there’s two types of cheaters:

The rage hackers who make it painfully obvious.
The “soft cheaters” trying to act sneaky while everyone fighting them KNOWS they’re cheating anyway.

But unless a cheater spins in circles, flies through the sky, and personally emails the devs saying “HELLO I AM CHEATING,” apparently there’s not enough proof.

Meanwhile these dudes survive wipe after wipe for YEARS untouched.

At this point the anti-cheat feels like a scarecrow sitting in a field hoping birds respect the rules voluntarily.

I swear half the PvP experience now is:
Die to cheater → switch server → die to another cheater → switch again → meet his cousin.

God I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hate this game because underneath all this garbage it’s actually fun.

after 300+ hours in this amazing game i see that the problem itself is not the devs or the game performance.

ITS THE CHEATERS, there is so many cheater in this game that tarkov starts to be an option again. 

i play gzw everyday and i see atleast 2 cheaters every singel day some of them are just ragecheating and killing the whole lobby instant and some of them are just wallhacking and using some kind of grassremoval cheat. i personaly think that its sad that a game for 100+ dollars do not have an Cornell level anti-cheat or any anti-cheat at all. Im not a hater do not get me wrong but this state of the game is unplayable for people that are not using cheats. I want this game to become playable for the fact that the Idea of the game is genius and i love the idea of not escaping the map having bases and all that stuff but plsssssss we NEED and better anti-cheat like Riot have in valorant and league. 

i hope someone importent to the game see a post like this 

Every move matter.

I picked up this game for some relaxed PvE rounds; graphically, it looked quite decent at first glance, but the gameplay feels clunky here and there—much of it still seems unoptimized and unfinished. The game is bogged down by an excessive number of mechanics you have to constantly keep track of, and the animations take an agonizingly long time to play out.

Enemies are either one-shot kills, soak up bullets like sponges, or simply one-shot *you*. The endless back-and-forth helicopter travel—combined with the fact that you can't simply extract from an unsecured zone—turns the whole experience into a tedious chore when all you want is to squeeze in a quick round.

The game's pacing needs to be significantly sped up; right now, it feels too sluggish and boring. It just didn't click with me. The graphics also seem very inconsistent: you might enter an area where everything looks high-resolution, only to encounter scenes moments later that look like something out of *Arma 2*, complete with pixelated visuals.

I'm not sure exactly how to categorize this game; as it stands, it doesn't appeal to me and still feels heavily unfinished. I might check back in at a later date, though - it definitely still has potential.

Good game, but very little gameplay... AND A LOT OF CHEATERS. Last 3 times I got ended by someone coming to me (while I literally lied in the bushes in the forrest) by one-two shots. That's not luck or skill. They exactly knew where I am.

Gray Zone Warfare: A Beautiful Disaster Built on a Foundation of Bad Choices
If you've ever wanted to experience what it's like to be a Navy SEAL operating in a lush, breathtaking jungle while simultaneously questioning every life decision that led you here—Gray Zone Warfare is your game.
The visuals? Chef's kiss. The atmosphere? Dripping with tension. The game engine holding it all together? Ah yes, Unreal Engine—the same digital playground that's been exploited, reverse-engineered, and cheated on more than any engine in existence. MADFINGER Games looked at this storied history of exploitation and said: "You know what this needs? Competitive PvP." Truly visionary stuff.
It's a bit like deciding to host a diamond collection in a cardboard box because the cardboard looks really nice on the outside.
And into that cardboard box walked every cheater with a $15 software subscription and a deep psychological need to feel superior at video games. These are the guys who spent their lunch money on an aimbot instead of actual skill development, and brother, it shows—just not in the way they think it does. Getting laser-beamed through dense foliage from 400 meters by someone with the spatial awareness of a golden retriever and the accuracy of a military satellite isn't intimidating. It's just sad. For them.
The cheating ecosystem here didn't appear out of nowhere—it was practically invited. Building a PvP experience on Unreal Engine without an anticheat solution worthy of the name is less of an oversight and more of an open casting call. MADFINGER essentially built a luxury car, forgot to install locks, then expressed surprise when people started joyriding. The cheaters didn't stumble into Gray Zone Warfare by accident. They read the engine selection and marked it in their calendars.
Every match becomes a quiet guessing game you never wanted to play. You take a carefully considered position, move with patience and discipline, execute a textbook approach—and get instantly deleted by someone who paid for the privilege of skipping the part where you actually learn the game. There's a particular kind of frustration reserved for dying to someone who couldn't tell you what button sprints if you asked them at gunpoint.
And then there's the AI. Rather than program enemies with believable behavior, intelligent positioning, or tactical awareness—you know, actual game design—MADFINGER apparently opened the difficulty settings, cranked the health bar to somewhere between "tank" and "geological formation," slapped on some extra armor, and called it a day. That isn't game design. That isn't challenge. That's a slider. A child could do it. A intern on their first day could do it. It requires exactly zero creative thought and represents the bare minimum of effort disguised as difficulty.
Dump your finest M995 AP rounds—the best ammunition the game offers, the stuff that costs a small fortune to source—into an NPC's chest cavity and watch him shrug it off like you threw a strongly worded letter at him. You, meanwhile, catch a single round to the forearm and immediately begin the dying process. The asymmetry isn't brutal game design. It's what happens when a developer mistakes "we made them harder to kill" for "we made them more interesting to fight." They are not the same thing. They have never been the same thing.
The bones of something brilliant are absolutely here. The map is stunning, the gunplay is weighty and satisfying, and those rare moments of genuine squad-based tactics feel like exactly the game MADFINGER promised. But those moments drown beneath a PvP environment compromised by cheaters who were essentially handed an engraved invitation, and AI enemies whose only notable trait is how many bullets they can absorb before acknowledging your existence.
MADFINGER chose the path of most exploitation, built a competitive mode on top of it, waved at the cheaters on their way in, and filled the world with bullet-sponge NPCs when writing real AI proved too inconvenient.
Pretty to look at. Painful to play. Impossible to trust.
Graphics & World Design: 9/10 — Genuinely gorgeous. No notes.
Core Gunplay: 7/10 — Satisfying, when your opponent is bound by the laws of physics.
AI Design: 1/10 — A health bar does not a challenge make.
PvP Integrity: 0/10 — A suggestion more than a feature.
Engine Choice for Competitive PvP: 0/10 — Bold. Catastrophically, hilariously bold.
Overall: 3/10 — A beautiful house built on a swamp, then handed the keys to the swamp creatures.

Bought Supporter Edition as soon as I saw the gameplay, it was on a discount and really wanted to see this work.

Turns out, 70-ish fps on medium on a RTX 5070 ti with a 9950X3D, nope, thank you. 

Will come back once performance is fixed, I'm not gonna play a PvPvE (or a PvP one at all) game with frame gen.

The game is good but after 20+ hours worth of grinding stuff, I fell off a mountain due to the game pushing me off, I died on a rock that was almost completely inaccesable, and when I went back to get my stuff (which you only have 60 minutes and 1 chance to do), I got stuck in some terrain and couldn't get out. I put in a support ticket and asked on the discord if someone could help me get unstuck. The discord chat had an ai that responded with an ai generated ticket on some random website, where my request was considered "high priority." I never got a word back in what has been over 90 minutes. All my stuff is gone now; thanks for nothing devs.

Edit: 6 days later, not a word (Keep in mind, the devs suggested putting in a ticket to help get unstuck)

If Steam allowed mixed or maybes for a recommendation, I would pick that. Gun customization and the character damage model are cool. I wouldn't mind the medical system becoming more advanced and difficult, but it is better than what most games offer. That said, the gameplay loop is a lake as deep as a puddle. If you are playing PvE, you accept a task, walk somewhere only to find it has been completely looted and all enemies killed, press use on some dead person or very non-obvious piece of paper or set piece, then complete the mission or return to a FOB/base and then complete the mission. Additionally, tasks are now given randomly, meaning if you play with a group no one will have the same tasks. If you manage to get lucky enough to share a mission, progression is not shared amongst everyone. I like the new task UI showing the areas where to look on the map, however it is extremely cluttered in its current iteration. Vendor progression is very unrewarding and unevenly balanced. Three of the six vendors have tasks that offer a decent amount of reputation to level them, however the remaining three have few and what little missions they have offer barely any reputation. This has resulted in people farming all the high level POIs to sell the loot acquired to speed level vendors.

Enemy AI is now more retarded than Greta Thunberg. They no longer are able to see or detect you beyond 100m (give or take). They do not advance on you, take cover or attempt to preserve their life. They stand in place and will let you pick them off one by one. It's very engaging gameplay. At the same time, they are also unable to hit from a few feet away...unless we're talking about when you are running; then they will hit you from damn near 50m and perfectly headshot you. Enemies with MP5s and Skorpions will turn you into mincemeat and paint your lower extremities in a way that will make Monet jealous; but once again they can't hit ♥♥♥♥ up close and will laser you from a distance.

Bullet penetration is still all over the place. Rounds that should penetrate a certain armor rating will be stopped, even with optimal rifle setups (for example, M855 out of a 20" barrel being stopped by IIIA from not even 25m away). Enemies don't play by the same rules as you. They can tank absurd amounts of damage and will only die from direct heart/brain hits or exsanguination. 

PvP is fun at times, however like every shooter there are a decent amount of cheaters and teamkilling to steal gear seems like it is at an all time high. 

With all of that said, the community (for the most part) make the game and experience worth it. I have enjoyed this update---outside of missions---more than the previous ones. I certainly hope the devs can get things more in tune and get some of the game turned around.

PS, fix tactical wallets being broken as ♥♥♥♥ and allowing people to carry ♥♥♥♥ that isn't money. And also make Tiger Bay not make my system scream bloody murder.

Not recommended. 

Game mechanically solid and has great potential. 

Utterly infested with a cheater problem. And we're not talking subtle, probably just a damn good player type of event. We're talking being shot through buildings type events. Devs either lazy or incompetent and seem entirely unwilling to invest even a basic effort to combat the issue.

It's good (so far), and I love the gameplay loop and with it having no timers in raid, enemies respawning say every 30 minutes or so, but I genuinely wish it wasn't very heavy with its' requirements. Sure, you could make the evasive answer that "oh it's an early access game" but, I don't think it needs parts that are THAT juiced.

I guess it's because of UE5? Honestly the only excuse I could think of, with it having to force Lumen being on, as well as some other Unreal Engine bullshittery. Admittedly my specs aren't the best, though.

Somehow the *other* extraction shooter runs better, which is my takeaway from this. It's unfortunate.

too many players per server (48)

run around a dead empty map picking up leftovers

i would start a questline and do 3-4 quests and tons of POIs with no enemies (just dead bodies) and empty containers (already looted)

stretches of 30+ minutes with no action

when you do actually get to play, it can be a ton of fun
So much fun, I love the devs too solid team and the future of this game is very bright.

Just a big time waster. Go here, grab this, tag this, etc. Game recycles lame missions that you'll have to repeat just to unlock better gear. PVP is horrid. Lame camping, can't see anyone, weird mechanics, AI that ruins it. Don't waste your money on this quarter baked game. Has some potential but is currently terrible.
Cant play PvP due to CHEATERS... Cannnot recommend at this stage but overall nice game (v0.4.2)
In a sea of bad games, this stands out right now. It's not perfect, but it's at least SOMETHING that I can enjoy. I wish the AI was more aggressive, but it isn't terrible.

The only thing that feels bad right now is the character movement. It feels too stiff/ugly and not smooth enough. Please improve the movement, animations, turning, running, and overall character control, because this would make the game much better.
enjoying not having a match timer, or a zone to outrun. just leave base and survive.

Bought this game at launch still managed to put a few hours in even with the launch issues. Coming back to try it all again. game has done a completely 180. Its an amazing game and amazing concept. it runs wonderfully. i havent had a single issue since returning. 10/10 do recommend. Cant wait to see where this game goes in the future. Community is also so nice and helpful. Cant complain at all

Let me start off by saying I absolutely love the concept behind this Arma/Tarkov love child. The PVP feels incredible(when legit) and the AI have been getting massive improvements and are quite challenging and a lot less buggy. I have only had the game for about a month at the time of posting this but it has quickly become my favorite game(I'm a sucker for EA pain). With all the good things I can say.. there is one thing that totally nullifies all of this.. THE CHEATER PROBLEM. I swear it must be like Tarkov where something like 60-70% of the games you load into FOR SURE have a cheater in them. I can only stay engaged in PvE for so long before I start to get bored of the repetitive nature. I love this game and the progress that is being made with all of these updates but I don't even want to play until the cheating is resolved to some degree..

I have been having so much fun with this game and it's quickly become my favorite game and what I launch as soon as I sit at my computer. I appreciate how the Devs are always improving and implementing changes based on community feedback.

Some things I will say which will most likely outdate this review at some point but task sharing for squads. This is a feature that would make a lot of people happy. Along with end game content, It's nice that you can get there pretty quickly, but it gets looted out so quickly that sometimes you can't get the boss kill you need for a while. Or you can't get certain gear your looking for unless you get there on a fresh server which isn't guaranteed. Though with how things have been from the Devs I'm sure there will be fixes to come.

One of my favorite things that I've experienced in this game is how nice people generally are. I only play PVE because it's what got me into the game, and the amount of help I've received from people from any faction is awesome. I've had people unlock doors for me when I didn't have the key, I've had people drop me loot, I've even had a small group form up at base camp to trade and give away items. There's also been times where I have been the person to help out with knowing where a task item is and getting someone on the right track. I love this community. Well, except for all the cheaters I hear about from PVP.

I will continue to play this game for a long while, I hope. And until I decide to log off for the last time, I hope to see what comes next and run into more cool people.

posting a warning to amd gpu users especially those with 90XX cards. Us users are seeing 40-50 fps, lag/stuttering, and heavy ghosting even with low settings. There are minor work arounds small improvements but its still rather unacceptable if you play other newer titles or are a pvper.

Nobody knows when this will be fixed.
Great - its coming along so well. Other early access games should aspire to be like this one.  These Devs are doing it right.
Fantastic game. Updates are in the right direction. A couple times killed by a single shot from ai that shouldn't have been possible.

fun and immersive world with good gunplay and the pve is perfect if youre looking for  a 24/7 running world  tactical shooter my only real complaint is the game needs more optimization and for some reason at least on AMD if you turn on frame gen it ignores youre fps cap i have mine set to 120 and the game pushes all the way up into the 140s for some reason which i guess would be good but my monitor is only 100hz 1440p

The fact that i have to spend 120 dollars to have inventory space is wild
This game is pure euroslop
Stay away
Gayet güzel ve potansiyele sahip bir oyun, türü seviyorsanız düşünmeden alın ama henüz Erken Erişim'de olduğunu unutmayın daha pişmesi gerek :)
A realistic mil sim that's improving for the better and it's worth every penny.

The first 38 hours of the game were great. I'm not a big PvP player, and the PvE is pretty engaging. Recently, though, I cannot play without the game crashing seemingly over and over (4 or 5 times in 3 minutes at some points). I know my system isn't like, absolutely top-tier, but GPU load crashing with 16gb of VRAM and a current-gen GPU shouldn't be an issue. I have submitted a ticket for a fix, but at this point, the game is completely unplayable for me. It seems that there are some similar issues on the discord page, but nobody seems to have gotten a resolution.

Gray Zone Warfare absolutely slaps. It's like Tarkov but for people that are fully employed or have families. The environments, weather systems, gun customization systems, audio design, character and item models, animations, and attention to detail all stand out and deserve to be recognized. Gray Zone brands itself as a PVE-first tactical shooter, and the game reflects this with the primary gameplay loop revolving around looting and PVE only tasks. It's about the journey you experience in Gray Zone, not the destination. Being able to turn off PVP is a game changer, and the biggest reason to buy this game if you are someone with no interest in PVP threats or have consistent life obligations that prioritize your time.

The barrier to entry is significantly cheaper and easier compared to other high profile games of the same genre ($55.00 CAD compared to $115.00 CAD for Tarkov and the PVE mode) and does not require thousands of hours to learn everything about the game. No Xsolla. No email domain or account creation issues. No separate launcher. Madfinger Games puts the community and player base first, and encourages open constructive feedback with tons of player requested development decisions consistently committed to and/or implemented into the game. The player community is very welcoming and wholesome, with people sometimes going out of their way to help new or struggling players. Server stability is very good for an early access title, and bugs/glitches are very minimal to non-existent in my playtime.

Squad play is totally optional, but unlike in ARC Raiders where playing with 1 or 2 other people forces you to play in shoot on sight PVP lobbies with other duo and trio queuing squads whether you are wanting to PVP or not, you can still play strictly PVE, regardless of the number of squad members. Granted the task management for squads is currently a problem with not being able to share tasks or task progress between teammates, but the developers have confirmed that a fix/rework is coming in a future patch before 0.5. Just remember to pick the same PMC faction as your friends in order to play together.

Playing in PVE only servers does have its downsides like getting robbed of boss kill contracts, empty PMC crates at COPS, and the frequent lack of loot in high tier areas, but this didn't happen as often as the community claims it does and the overall experience you are getting with the PVE only mode, without having to think about cheaters or players with 1000+ hours, vastly out weighs these negatives. Implementing either smaller player capacity on PVE servers or icons on your map for every player location on the server regardless of faction on PVE servers only, could help with the lack of loot in Ter 3 areas most commonly mentioned in player feedback. Task systems could be adjusted as well, with main story tasks being more linear to better understand the organization of the story, as it can become quite convoluted without any direction given to the player, being able to choose whatever main task you want in whatever order you want. The actual story itself is quite interesting if read into and teases what will come in the future updates and eventual release of the full game.

The best thing about Gray Zone is simply the option to play the mode you want, and the developers need to be commended for giving the player the ability to choose PVEVP or PVE, instead of making that choice for you. As it is not even in its beta state yet, the opportunities for this game are vast, and it has major potential to become an incredible title. Highly recommended and worth the price.

love this game. I have only played it for 15 hours and i am pretty hooked. If you love a fps, realistic and intense game this is easily one of the best choise. Developers are really doing a good job with continuously adding stuff to the game. You always get to experience something new. And you really need to put some hours to get the hang of it. I have been playing fps games since i was a kid , I can easily vouch for this game as one of the best ones I have played. 

Best thing I personally love about this game is that you can't shortcut your way through, meaning, you have to explore everything alone or with your friends, you have to actually think as if you are really there IRL or you gonna get shot from somewhere, you have to be patient, you have to think when to pull the trigger. Its really fun Trust Me.

Unless you like to risk your hardware I'd avoid this game like a Hantavirus Cruise Liner.

Where to start, where to start... 100c CPU temps for a start.

Disabled boost brought my CPU temps down to 55c-60c in this game.

But if you disabled your CPU boost the base clock speed and core count on my CPU is enough to run this game like a peach without melting components. - The game with boost enabled seems to kick to 5.4Ghz and just hold there, no walking up or down as required to process the area adequately no clock speeds dropping below full boost. Yet the game runs far better and smoother with higher FPS when this feature is disabled. This is the only game I have ever experienced a problem like this on. Some players don't seem to have this, and are a vocal minority who show up to leave the usual "it works for me it must be your PC that's the problem", the majority I have spoken too seemingly also have a similar issue. Yet some people with the exact same CPU are reporting no issues.

I run this game on High settings or above, with processor performance boost set to "disabled" in the power plan options. This is was required even with a 360mm AIO cooler with 6 fans,  three intake and three exhaust fans either side plus the case has another 6 fans installed. am consistently above 80 FPS. 

Well for one the gameplay is at most, stale, the repetitive nature of the quests isn't the problem. The constant need to be drinking every 10 seconds is ridiculous I don't need a pathetic sound repeating ad nauseum to remind me. 

I will say this game is so poorly coded that I have to disabled my CPU boost because for some reason this game wants to melt CPU's now 100c is within safe operating temps for my CPU as defined by the MfG but that level of optimisation is beyond pathetic. 

Actually had a "forum moderator" tell me that disabling the boost would cause problems, it's fixed their incompetent mess of a game sadly....

Bios is updated for 14th gen, cooling is adequate.

Game is so poorly coded I'd consider broken.

Don't get me wrong, I love the game play who wouldn't enjoy irons on an M16A1 through the jungles of SEA.

Very fun game, if you enjoy looting and doing quests then you can hop onto pve and do that. If you enjoy pvp more, you can hop onto the pvp server and fight players or also do quests and looting. It feels like there are many different ways to play the game depending on the experience you want. I really love this game. 

As a message for the developers, I would like to have more customization options for the character like being able to play as a female character, and a few more varieties of gear that can be found in the game. It would also be cool if there was a community board in the base where players can post quests or missions or events for other players, so like you could post that you want to raid Midnight Sapphire at 8 pm and people can know that easily by looking at the community board. I can't wait to see how this game will further develop!

I think I jumped into Gray Zone Warfare at exactly the right time with Update 0.4 "Spearhead."

What hooked me the most is the looting. I love searching for valuable items, filling my backpack, and trying to make it back to extraction alive. Whether I'm actively looking for PvP or avoiding other players while carrying expensive loot, the game creates a great high-risk, high-reward experience that keeps every run exciting.

The map is massive and visually impressive. Exploring different areas never feels repetitive, and the graphics make the world feel immersive despite the game still being in development.

I'm also excited about the future of the game. The developers have mentioned adding a hideout system and crafting mechanics in Update 0.5, which could add even more depth to progression and give players additional goals beyond missions and PvP.

The game still has room to grow, but based on my experience with 0.4, I'm looking forward to seeing where Gray Zone Warfare goes next.

Game crashes a TON on Linux systems. The gameplay is fun and all, but crashing and loosing all your stuff because you can't log in without crashing is remarkably irritating. And running it on the lowest settings doesn't help.

Put in a refund request, and I'm hoping it goes through, I don't have the money to spend on games I cannot play. Though I am looking forward to what the Dev's do in the future though, so I'll be following the game's progress.

And if the Dev's are listening, please let me know when you got the Linux compatibility running smoothly, I'd gladly come back to playing the game then. And a more personal request, would you kindly add the AR10 to the game? And give it waffle magazines? I'd greatly appreciate it.

They ruined the game.
Squads starting fresh together doesn't get the same quests even in the same factions.
Movement is not smooth.
They give you quests out in the field that cannot be completed while out in the field.

I really wish I could recommend this game, or at least give it a "Maybe" rating.  It's got a gorgeously detailed environment, the weapon customization is awesome, loadout choices matter, and the tension of approaching an enemy stronghold solo and firing and displacing as you work through their troops is palpable.  It scratches that real-world looter-shooter itch just right.  I've easily got 1000+ hours in tactical FPS games across 20 years now, and very few of those have given me the same intense feelings of danger and excitement of ambush, battle and escape that this game has.  They're putting together something pretty great...

... EXCEPT for several glaring and persistent issues that for me completely RUIN what would otherwise be a very immersive and well-made experience:

1) NPCs are very frequently aimbot terminators with thermal-vision auto-targeting Raybans against players firing suppressed from carefully-selected concealed positions 150m away that no human would be able to detect a threat from before he and all his friend humans around him were dead from measured, accurate fire, or were cowering in cover from.  Nope, instead within seconds they have somehow spotted that well-concealed and suppressed player through the foliage and intervening terrain, raised their rusty AK with iron sights and put sniper-accurate fire onto the player in a handful of seconds, landing grievous wounds or headshots.  I'm former military, and these AI reactions are just completely nonsense.  Real humans, even well-trained, would take many seconds to figure out even which rough direction suppressed shots were coming from, then have to pop their heads out to scan for very-hard-to-spot-in-daytime muzzle flash or foliage/dust disturbance, then peer a moment longer to be sure of that small indication they saw, THEN start trying to put fire on their enemy's firing location, with their first shots likely not being at the right range either.  They'd likely die at one of those stages by the incoming fire, or they'd stay cowering and make their enemy either come flush them out or withdraw - and that would be preferred, it's more believable and it's fun to have to go in an get the more intelligent NPCs out from cover in CQB action, repositioning and coming in at another angle, etc.  These AI however usually skip all that and just spot you and engage you immediately like they're an Apache gunner on PCP, it's ridiculous.  The other times they just stand still and look around confused before running to something that is very much not cover is much more realistic (if also still unfun) than their Star-Wars-Bounty-Puck-homing on you capabilities they otherwise have.

2) NPCs are also bullet sponges; there's nothing like that feeling of getting gunned down by an aimbot NPC you didn't even see 100m down the road in the dark despite taking everything very cautiously and checking all your corners and sightlines and everything (hey, that CAN happen IRL), and having to do a corpse run with just your pistol (since you're planning on putting all your original gear back on from your body) and coming in very quiet and stealthy.. and running into a lone literally-shirtless NPC gunman who you promptly put 5 rounds center-mass into his shirtless, unarmored body only to watch him grunt, duck behind a tree, and then pop out at you and drill you with his AK.  Was I firing paintballs?  ONE round through his sternum from my Glock should have put him in the dirt, nevermind half my mag.  Nope, instead shirtless terminator guns you down AGAIN with more aimbot precision and now you can't loot your previous corpse at all anymore, despite coming back a 3rd time with full gear again, mag-dumping on everything that glances at you, and then standing over your own body still clad in your own now-unlootable previously-favorite gear.  WTF "death is a punishment" design is that in a PVE where dying by aimbot is not only possible but the likely manner in which you'll go down?

3)  Keys unlock already-looted rooms most of the time because other players have already been there and the server hasn't reset or picked another location for that key - yes, I know the keys are to specific places, but there could be different doors in those places with just a bit more work on level design to prevent a player going to the exact same room that another player just cleaned out 15 minutes prior.  Oh, and all the NPCs on the way for your mission are still dead because of that other player doing that run 15 minutes ahead of you - or they spawn in behind you and are waiting to aimbot ambush you on your way out through the area that was just clear 2 minutes ago; hey, that's partially realistic, patrols rove and new shifts arrive - but there's no sense of timing of their arrival often enough, especially for having just cleared a dry hole, and that makes it unfun.

4)  Cheaters.  It could be #1 on this list, because it's egregiously bad, but the Devs don't seem to care. I haven't even gotten into the PVP content myself because of what I've seen of it on Twitch, but even just watching 3rd-party the cheaters are numerous and their accounts never seem to get deleted. For how long this game's been in "Early Access" the Devs should have handled this by now - makes me want to NEVER even bother with PVP at all then.

I really wish I could recommend this game, it's got SO much potential.  But these issues persist over a year now that I've tried it off and on and now I'm about to uninstall (again) - so I figured I'd share my thoughts.

If I see the Devs fix the AI so they at least behave mostly like humans maybe I'll come give it another go.

Do you like military sims and extraction shooter type games, but hate dealing with cheaters? Just get this game and stay on PVE and its great! I have not even tried PVP yet due to so much content to explore in PVE. Did I mention the game has beautiful graphics including realistic rain ect...?

This is a fantastic game backed by great developers, and it’s incredibly solid for an alpha. I started in the 0.4 preview and have clocked over 500 hours since; I don't regret a single second and still want more.

I recommend it 100%, but I've hit a wall that makes me hesitate to launch the game just for fun now that I'm level 50 and have cleared the main tasks and contracts. Minor bugs aside, the biggest issue right now is player density.

Currently, other players completely vacuum up the loot and wipe out the AI that it’s incredibly difficult to find a live zone—even just to get 10 kills for a task. While having people around during the initial leveling process made the world feel alive and actually helped speed up questing, the endgame experience is opposite.

With maxed-out vendors and no major goals left, focus naturally shifts to exploration, studying lucrative POIs, optimizing routes, and learning enemy spawns. Unfortunately, having every major location stripped clean of both AI and loot 90% of the time kills the fun of late game. It’s still amazing to jump in and help friends who are still leveling, but when playing solo, I now find myself choosing to watch a show or work during my downtime instead of logging into GZW. The core loop is already solid, but the endgame desperately needs some form of private/local servers with no other people. 

The PvE balance of the game is brilliant: a single operator can systematically clear an entire POI of both its AI population and valuable loot. This creates an incredibly satisfying loop. As a solo player, it feels challenging (at least for me) yet entirely achievable; as a squad, you feel an empowering tactical advantage. Both dynamics feel great.

However, because a single player can strip a location clean, it only takes one person arriving 3 to 10 minutes ahead of you to completely ruin your entire experience and you have to wait for respawns, and during that time someone else is going in, then someone else, then an entire new squad rolls up. Honestly, the most fun I had in the game was during the early weeks when the majority of the player base was still leveling. Reaching Tier 2 and Tier 3 POIs ahead of the curve meant that 90% of the time, I had the zones to myself. This allowed me to map out enemy spawns, optimize my clearing routes, farm effectively, and eventually teach those strategies to my friends.

Now that the broader player base has progressed into Tier 2 and Tier 3 content, these zones have completely lost their depth. They are no longer viable for exploration or lucrative looting; they’ve essentially been reduced to basic quest hubs where you just run in to interact with an objective and leave.

holy poop, anti cheat not loading up error, even Tarkov lets you load into the game... spent over 3hrs fixing it, but no luck...

after another at least 3hrs i got to the solution.. it was my name on steam

so far I like it

Love this game. I really like the fact that the developers made two modes. one for pvp and one for pve. Having the choice is really nice. you dont always have time for pvp or just not in the mood. Sometimes you are. Giving the player  and money spending customer options is a great thing.

I love this game, but unfortunately there is an epidemic of cheaters. Almost every single time I've logged on in the past week, I have encountered blatant cheating. Aimbots, wall hacks, radar. MFG needs to do something about it. Raise the minimum pc requirements. Use a kernel level anti-cheat. AND FOR THE LOVE OF GOD--MAKE BANS PERMANENT. Who cares if you hurt the cheaters feelings, BAN FOREVER. They suck the fun right out of the game.

The game is fun, the guns feel great and I will keep playing it more. I am purely a PVE player, I just wanted a milsim ghost recon game, which this is. But, currently the harder missions are done by everyone, which means most of the time you will find a zone already looted with all the enemies dead. It always feels meh and I feel like this one thing takes away from the experience. It would be great if there was a way to work around this, like private servers or so. I don't really care for the loot, but going into a high danger area, just to see everyone dead never feels good.

The PVE is good and has a lot of potential. However the servers are over crowded and its impossible to do anything without running into a bunch of people. Not very fun when everywhere you go all the enemies are already dead

Didn't think I would like it, seemed like a Tarkov ripoff, but it is not. PVEPVP is really fun, gunplay is fun, the open world and no raid timers is fun. This is nothing like Tarkov except maybe the weapon modding detail. Also the Dev's seem to actually want their players to enjoy the game, unlike Tarkov where the Dev's hate the player base and do whatever they can to make the game worse. It needs work and fleshing out for sure, but this is already a solid game and scratches the extraction shooter itch with less focus on the extraction part and more focus on the shooter part. Also PVE is nice because sometimes I want a game to play where I don't get one tapped by a no life unemployed with 20k hours and nothing better to do.

It's 2026, I've had the game since alpha launch (2 years 1 week ago) and the Devs are definitely listening and cooking - super rare in this day and age of gaming.

If you want a challenge with tactical flare, jump on board. At the very least, stay tuned.

Only downside is that the Devs are only human and can't pump out the end-game fast enough, unlocking more of the lore & story of Lamang Island and the reason to be popping and looting the various enemy factions.

Absolutely loving it! 
Amazing mix of milsim and just a bit action shooter, getting hit by a bullet is really a pain.

Love how you can customize your guns and even build them yourself, running custom guns non stop.

Looting feels good and combat is getting better with every update and the map just looks amazing.
Bought this game three days ago and I’m hooked! I’ve been hearing about this game for months. This is the first real game ive played on PC. On sale too so why not get it?
The Devs listen to the community,  improvements are consistently being made. community is cool.

Imagine the map and gameplay-loop of Ghost Recon Wildlands in a bowl, sprinkle in the inventory/vendor system from Tarkov with the gunplay of Ground Branch, then add a dash of the injury mechanics from MGS3 Snake Eater. Mix until smooth, then slow cook for two years.

Absolute. F***ing. Cinema.

What a game. I played on release when I had a 2080 Super and the optimization was really not where I wanted it to be. After 2 years of waiting I can say that the Devs have not let up and this game is great. When a tactical shooter can give you this much immersion and also horror game vibes when running at night solo..... "Chef's kiss"

AI is still WAY overtuned in jungle areas. They even made them have laser accuracy. Suppressors do nothing at all. Even at very far ranges at night and sniping with a low-caliber suppressed rifle, ALL enemies in their vicinity automatically lock on to you even if you reposition very slowly, and don't let go of aggro until you die.
The core of the game is great. BUT!!!! keys need to be more rare and not unlimited use, also as a pvp main the cheaters have made the game unplayable for me.

REMOVED FROM STEAM LIBRARY.
After discovering that the game developers are selling the cheats that make the game not playable for the user base - Im requesting a refund to the full amount of the game - because no where in there license or service agreement does it say that as a game player that i'll be farmed by other humans using aim bots and ESP's to loot my fake in game items so they can be sold into in game currency which is then used for real world money laundering.

NO WHERE IN THE USER AGREEMENT DOES IT SAY IM A WILFUL PARTICIPANT TO MONEY LAUNDERING FOR A GAME DEVELOPMENT STUDIO... AND I'D LIKE MY ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ MONEY BACK.

First impressions of this game were quite poor. Right out of the gate, no matter what settings or online "fixes" I tried, the anti aliasing looks like absolute ♥♥♥♥. I'm talking BAD bad. The game runs poorly without DLSS and looks like a smudgy nightmare with it on. On top of that, both the movement and menus feel super clunky as someone used to Tarkov.

I would not recommend this product.
Early Access for two years, the game still crashes frequently even with higher end components. Let this game cook a while longer before spending the money on it. It ain't ready yet.
The best and highest-quality game I've ever played. Highly recommended for anyone who loves a high-quality, relaxed, and tactical PvE game!

If you plan to buy this game for PvE mode - it doesn't worth it. There's only 1 loot instance per server, which is affected by random spawn chance + all other players on that server. All major locations - Midnight Sapphire (Hotel and Villas), Tiger Bay, Fort Narith - are empty 9/10 times. After the recent twitch streams initiative, there's a wave of loot goblins, who block the other players from getting in/out of rooms as "punishing" and also rush towards the loot while you still fighting the enemies. Developers are "aware" of this issue, but that means absolutely nothing until this issue is fixed. The game is supposed to be hard, yes. But it's not supposed to be hard due to the people's toxicity and salty behavior. Being a PvE-first game, it's funny that the loot system is not fairly balanced (in PvE). Regardless of enemies/loot respawn timers, the same players just sitting on the same spots (e.g. Tiger Bay) and farming loot over and over, while any other players who recently joined in are screwed. The only option is to either a) server-hop (which is a crutch instead of a solution), or b) play PvP (this is valid, but not everybody wants to play PvP after a hard day of work, but still wants to enjoy the Lamang). Since I prefer the PvE over PvP, I cannot recommend this game right now. Hard pass.

Was fun until a hacker spawn camped the base camp. Then threw me in an eternal loop of death. I couldnt even logout, I had to force stop the game just to leave. Reported the guy like 50 times, and nothing happened. Very disappointed, especially since once youve died more than once before getting your geear its lost. So all my good gear was lost to a hacker.

Under Linux I have to verify every second time otherwise the anticheat prohibits, that it runs.
Performance is worse than a year ago. with medium graphic settings I barely get 30fps.

it got even Worse with the latest patch I'm now down below 10 Frames
Haven't touched this game since release and its come a LONG way. My biggest compliant was the vague task system having no idea where to go relying on googling each task to just find where tf to go.

A game i've really enjoyed up until recently. It has always been an issue but now more than before. There are way too many players on such a small map with extremely limited POI. You have maybe 6 areas to go to that are worth visiting unless doing tasks. Which will always be populated by teammates or enemy squad leaving virtually nothing for you to loot if that is what you are after. There has also been an odd task and with the way they changed it is not as easy to complete tasks alongside a partner. Idk, most likely just my own opinion but lately the game has been extremely boring and underwhelming as anywhere I go i never have to shoot since my team/enemy team has taken care of all ai and taken care of taking the loot. Spend like real minutes in transit to narith, start running towards it just to see people already extracting with everything or people looting the scraps.
The .4 Spearhead update definitely has made me grind this game like no other went from 10 hours to putting in over 100 hours.  Can definitely tell the developers truly care about the game
Retroactively updated the Anti-Cheat to ban VM's making it impossible to play on a Cloud PC except Geforce Now

Pretty based Operator PvPvE-Game without any lgbtq-h0m0-gay-sh!t or call of duty: woke ops! weapon skins.
No black lesbian girl boss characters or dialouge that won't hurt your gay ass sissy feelings.
10/10. Would operate again.

A slower, sloppier version of tarkov. Dead easy. Graphics look like someone placed a sharpness filter on a photo and then another sharpness filter... and then another one.

Game has NO MOB AI. None. They'll shoot, almost never hit you... it's dumb. 

The loot consists of lighters and lamp oil. You hunt down "thugs" and "mercanaries" but really they're fighting for freedom while ur just the blackrock dude set to kill them all.

It resets, so nothing you do really matters... It's like 4000gb to install, because of the sharpness filter, but every room is filled with really cool stuff that you in no way interact with.

The sound design is like someone throught about birds and leaves, but didn't allign them to anything and completely forgot they were making a shooting game. At the last minute they added footsteps bc "the boos said"... 

Really there is ZERO heart put into this game. IT's all graphics to sell numbers. Game is ass. Go play Incusion Red River.

the way these updates are coming along, this game is going in the right direction.

There are some QoL that should be addressed. But given its in early access, i think the devs know what they are doing.

A much more casual milsim extraction game with awesome graphics, engaging gameplay, and a dedicated PVE mode. 

Possitives:

Gunplay is very fun remincicent of Ready or Not with enemies reacting differently depending on where they are hit and what they are equiped with.
weapon customisation is very in depth (somtimes confusing but you get used to it with time)
World design and lore is engaging painting a dark and often downright disturbing (which i love)
quests are often complex (sometimes overly so) and respect the players intellegence and problem sovling. 
Enemy Ai have been well tuned and feel extremely human, pushing or pulling back or blindfiring into the jungle if they cant see you. (However they arent perfect yet)
Health and healing system is extremely in depth and very cool
Eating and drinking animations are very satisfying (just wish there were more)

Negatives:

Appearently hackers are a issue in PVP,  (This doesnt effect me much as i esclusivly play the PVE)
Bugs are suprisingly minimal but can be annoying or even gamebreaking at times. 
On occaision server/network lag are an issue however this is normaly quite infrequent and effects the game for only a second or two.
Enemy AI somtimes go into terminator mode and headshot you with ironsights from 700 meters away in the middle of the night while your crouched behind a bush...

Awesome Milsim Extraction shooter in early access with dedicated fully fleshed out PVE set in an immersive dark world. Minimal bugs, great gameplay,

Very good game. Devs are constantly updating and bugfixing. Very worth the money. Especially since it is way easier to get started in compared to Tarkov or Arena Breakout. And a very own PVE-Only mode for those who just want to do the quests/farming without interruptions. 

ONLY ONE THING. Pls let me have the option to join active/almost full PVP lobbies. Or prioritize filling lobbies. I sometimes have to search for a active PVP lobby.
9/10 game. Hope for more content or special PVP events in the future. Keep going that way Madfinger games you are doing everything right.

Why is this not more popular! Devs that genuinely care about their game, listen to the community and make updates that actually show improvement. Unlike most other tactical extraction shooters, this one guides you just enough to where you know where to go for missions, but leaves out enough have to figure out what exactly you're looking for and why. The mechanics overall are very solid and more in depth than pretty much any other game in the same category. Easily one of the best to play alone or with friends, even coming across randoms in the PvE is a great time. PvP can be sweaty but that expected especially considering you can level up completely in the PvE and bring that gear into PvP. Given all of that I'd 100% recommend this game to anyone looking for a extraction shooter that feels realistic but not like you're always in no mans land. 
     Now with that info I have to clarify that this game does require a pretty decent setup to run at higher FPS rates but the devs have been releasing updates over time that have fixed many of these issues little by little. With time I truly think this has the potential to be the top game in the extraction shooter category.

lol I just got back with just a knife, so no gear, and I killed 7 agent players + 2 AIs on Arena Breakout Infinite in one game. In the previous game, I only killed 2 agent players and 8 AIs, and I managed to get through with max gear. Meanwhile, on Grayzon Warfare, I played 2 games and only encountered cheaters in PvP. What are you guys doing about it? lol I know Arena also has headshot aimbot cheaters like you, but sometimes you can breathe a sigh of relief there. lol Do something about PvP, you guys are crazy! That's why I quit your game today. World Wars 3, a game on Steam from 2022, is a realistic weapon sim like yours, does better in terms of anti-cheat and is fun right now.

The game has some potential but it's many glaring faults ruin the game play.  You have to spend money to get tracking tags so you can find you stuff when you die but they expire.  I died and went straight back to my body, there was nothing but blood on the ground where i bled out.  My body was gone and i lost all my stuff again.  I'm not going to start all over again for the third time.  I should have known better to waste my money on an early access game.

“The game has gotten so much better, with lots of improvements and quality-of-life changes, but at the current moment, for new players, unless you’re fine doing PvE only, I wouldn’t recommend it. For players looking for PvP, the game is becoming rampant with cheaters in every lobby, and no one is addressing it right now.”

“It sucks to say this because I really do love the PvP and the intense, random moments that happen, and I want this game to be my new main game since I have over 700 hours in it. But unless the devs start doing something about these cheaters, PvP servers are becoming almost unplayable. It’s insane how many times, in just two weeks, I’ve died at Midnight Sapphire or Harrison, respawned at main base, and had a couple guys walk up to me asking if I died to a certain player and having to server hop.”

Even playing between Windows and Linux, this game has had consistently poor performance random crashes that required re-installing EAC again and again after every boot. This is a game that has been out long enough and still fails to fix its most basic problems.

Regardless the guns and character customization is nice -- The way everything feels is nice and amazing.
obviously the game has a bit to improve on and a ways  off full release but it's still in a really good place and is quite fun to play be it pvp or the more relaxed pve mode

loved the game but the new task system? sheeesh, you could play all time in starter town and reach level 40 easy. 
also, all the tasks are just all the same? why do i get the same task 3 times in a row? 

nah, im waiting for the next wipe.

I can't give this a thumbs up. If the game crashes you cant have the revival point where you were last. This resulted in my character being killed (right when i logged back on btw) and almost lost everything. A crash happened just now in the middle of a firefight and I really don't want to know what happens to my guy anymore. It's exhusting. Not fun. Change this crap and i'll take down the bad review.

I’m giving a NEUTRAL-POSITIVE rating only because of the huge potential and the amazing game concept. The atmosphere is great, and the game is less hardcore than Tarkov – which is a plus, making it accessible not only to streamers but also to casual players (F.CK Tarkov!). So the prospects for GZW are promising.

Now the downsides: the game is just unplayable. I’m getting around 35–39 FPS almost all the time, and there’s really huge input LAG! I feel that the input lag is even worse than it should be at this low FPS.
Hardware: 9060XT, 5700X3D

Other new games run super smooth with FSR enabled. In Squad, I get 80–100 FPS all the time. In Tarkov, I also get 80 FPS on demanding maps, and 120 FPS on most others.

This is a known optimization issue for the Radeon 9000 series – and nothing has changed for over a month, even though the developers are supposedly aware of it. That’s disappointing.

I have 1440p resolution, FSR on PERFORMANCE, settings MEDIUM-HIGH (with a few EPIC settings that have low FPS impact).

Funny enough, my GPU is only using about 70% of its TDP, and changing graphics settings has almost ZERO impact on performance: I can set everything to EPIC, and FPS will still go from 35 to 39 in open areas.

This is THE GAME I was looking for :) Not sure how I missed it earlier.

Perfect balance between relaxing gameplay (PvE) and full-sweat mode (PvP), when you go to hunt for other players.

Guns? A lot of guns, equipment, options for customization - but without total mess of ARMA and all the mods to it.

Looks? Looks AWESOME (jungle in the storm...) and for now didn't noticed any performance problems (but I'm on high-end hw, so can't judge for everyone).

For now playing basic version of the game - but probably upgrade to some pack's: not for stuff in them, but just to support the devs. Can't wait what future will bring (note to devs: please don't f-this up - really love the game <3).

PS - to be fair: are there things to improve? Sure. Is some stuff missing? Sure. I would like to see some neutral / civilian population, to get some story depth and be able to save someone from time to time in the missions, instead just finding next body...
This game is so realistic and detailed I would consider using it as a reference for purchasing parts for my irl firearms. 10/10 :)

    Last thing but not as big of a deal is MORE GEAR but that will come with time im sure. Other than that this game is working to be something great.

great game if the lagging bugs got fixed i would never stop playing it

This game is awesome. The community is absolutely amazing and more than willing to help new players along. I highly recommend playing if you like Open World Shooters.

After 100 hours of gameplay, I’ve realized a few things about Gray Zone Warfare.

First of all, I understand that the game is still in Early Access, but with my RTX 3060, it’s clear that the game is not fully optimized yet. Even on High settings, my PC generates a lot of heat, and I still experience FPS drops in certain areas. Sometimes the frame rate can even fall to around 10–20 FPS. I hope the developers can address these performance issues soon and make the game run more smoothly.

Next is the gameplay. This is a survival and military simulation game, and for me, it took around 10–20 hours to get used to the mechanics. I was quite shocked to discover that when your character dies, you lose all of your gear and have to recover from scratch. On two occasions, I was disconnected from the regional server, and the game effectively reset my progress by wiping all of my weapons and equipment. However, after another 50 hours of gameplay, I realized that rebuilding your loadout is actually not too difficult once you understand the game.

Finally, one of the biggest positives for me is the community. During the times when I lost everything, many players helped me by giving me high-tier gear without expecting anything in return.

Overall, Gray Zone Warfare is an Early Access title that is developing in a positive direction. It requires time and patience to learn, but once you become familiar with its systems, the game becomes much more approachable. Playing with friends also adds a strong tactical element and makes the experience even more enjoyable.

I've been around Gray Zone Warfare since launch in April 2024 and had a blast with it early on. Came back for the Night Ops update in November, loved that too, then put the game down waiting for more content to fill out the world. Jumped back in when Spearhead update dropped this April and what a difference. The devs are really on a good path for these past two years.

Back at launch I was stuttering around with low FPS, same rig now I'm pushing 100+ without frame gen. Feels like the devs finally nailed optimization too.

The world feels way more alive now. There's so much going on in the map compared to before, AI actually makes sense instead of moving like a robot, and everything just clicks together better. I'm still playing PvE only so I can't comment on the whole cheater thing everyone's complaining about in PvP, but from my side the game is genuinely enjoyable now.

It's the kind of tactical shooter you can fire up, do some stuff for an hour or two, then hop off without any real stress. Super relaxing compared to what extraction shooters usually are. I've clocked a few hundred hours just on Spearhead alone and am still having fun. MFG has me hopeful for the future, this game's got legs if they keep pushing forward.
